title: Ruflo + Computer-Use Repo Static Clone/Inspect/Extract
clone_policy: Clones are git-ignored, never staged, never installed, never executed. Commit SHAs are recorded for reproducibility.

candidates.name: Ruflo
useful_patterns: Role-labeled single-purpose agents (each agent has one narrow job).
Shared local memory as the coordination substrate between agents.
Explicit coordinator/worker separation with task handoffs.
Plugin/skill registration so capabilities are declared, not implicit.
risks: Documented obfuscated malicious npm preinstall script (v3.1.0-alpha.55 through v3.5.2) that deleted directories.
MCP prompt-injection (Issue #1375): tool descriptions covertly added the maintainer as a repo contributor.
Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; not local-only; multiplies API spend across swarms.
Autonomous multi-agent swarms; broad MCP/action surface; documented Windows compatibility issues.
candidates.first_safe_next_test: Read-only source/dependency audit of package.json lifecycle scripts and MCP tool-description strings. No npm install, no MCP server start, no API key use.
candidates.notes: MIT permits reuse, but the supply-chain history means we re-express the architecture pattern from scratch (no code copied) rather than vendoring Ruflo source.

candidates.name: TryCUA / CUA Driver
candidates.source_evidence: Recorded across many committed n3-era audits (e.g. 14_context/cua_trycua_exact_source_evaluation.md, 14_context/codex_cua_trycua_source_crosscheck_n3_4.md).
useful_patterns: Observation -> plan -> action computer-use loop for desktop agents.
Containerized desktop sandbox (Docker / QEMU / KASM / Lumier) to isolate control.
Background 'driver' separating capability from policy on macOS/Windows.
risks: Partial checkout on Windows: some CI/workflow files with invalid-on-Windows names did not materialize; key files (README, LICENSE, package.json, pyproject.toml, Dockerfile) are present.
candidates.first_safe_next_test: Read README + pyproject.toml + Dockerfile read-only to map the agent loop. No Docker build/run, no driver start, no desktop control.
candidates.notes: Computer-use stays disabled by default behind a feature flag and global kill switch.

candidates.name: Browser Harness
candidates.source_evidence: Recorded in committed repo: 14_context/external_repo_clone_registry.json (status cloned, commit 4ec6255).
useful_patterns: Thin CDP websocket-to-Chrome harness (one socket, minimal layers).
Editable agent-workspace helpers the agent extends during a task.
risks: Direct live control of a real Chrome over CDP.
Self-modifying: the agent writes new helper code during execution.
Autonomy framing ('you will never use the browser again') conflicts with supervised gates.
candidates.first_safe_next_test: Read install.md and harness source read-only. Do NOT connect to Chrome, do NOT run, do NOT let it self-write code.
candidates.notes: Self-writing-code behavior must never be enabled inside Ghoti without strict approval gating.

candidates.name: Vercel agent-browser
candidates.source_evidence: Resolved from N+6.11A inventory alias 'vercel-labs/agent-browser'; URL reachable and cloned this milestone.
useful_patterns: Native Rust browser-automation CLI invoked as discrete commands.
Uses Chrome for Testing (pinned, downloaded) rather than the user's daily browser.
risks: Global npm install of a native binary; downloads a Chrome build on first run.
Live browser automation; binary distribution trust.
candidates.first_safe_next_test: Read the CLI command surface + Cargo.toml read-only. No global install, no Chrome download, no run.
candidates.notes: Apache-2.0 requires preserving NOTICE/attribution if any code is ever reused.

candidates.name: UI-TARS
candidates.source_evidence: Committed repo references UI-TARS only as 'intake/planning only' (e.g. 14_context/claude_n4_1f_*, N+5.0A observation work). No exact clone URL is recorded, and there are multiple distinct upstream repos (model vs desktop app).
useful_patterns: Vision-language GUI grounding (parse a screenshot into elements/actions).
Observation-only screen parsing (aligns with the N+5.0A observation adapter).
risks: Desktop control if run; large model weights; live API.
Ambiguous source: do not guess which upstream repo is intended.
candidates.first_safe_next_test: Operator supplies the exact source URL. Then static clone + license/README inspection only, no runtime.
candidates.notes: Per mission rule 'do not guess' - kept as a source-needed report rather than cloning a guessed URL.

candidates.name: Computer-use stack
candidates.source_evidence: Umbrella grouping; members tracked individually in this manifest.
useful_patterns: Observation-first screen parsing before any action.
Approval-gated action with a sandboxed desktop/browser.
Capability/policy separation so control is declared and gated.
risks: Desktop control, live browser, and container runtimes - all kept disabled by default.
candidates.first_safe_next_test: Treat each member separately under its own feature flag; keep computer-use disabled behind the global kill switch.
candidates.notes: No member is runtime-wired. The computer_use_enabled flag stays false.

candidates.name: Browser-use
candidates.user_priority_signal: referenced; blocked priority in N+6.11A inventory
useful_patterns: Python browser-agent framework with local and cloud modes.
risks: Cloud product emphasizes stealth / proxy / CAPTCHA-solving, which conflicts with Ghoti's no-bypass policy.
Consequential example flows (shopping, applications) need hard approval gates; external model API keys.
candidates.first_safe_next_test: Defer. If needed later, static-clone only the local open-source parts read-only after approval; never use stealth/CAPTCHA/proxy features.
candidates.notes: Not cloned this milestone (blocked priority). Documented for completeness.
